data.description:
## Story S7: Host shim compatibility error + plugin version bump

### Description
**What**: When a host project's `.claude/scripts/dso` shim is outdated and does not expose the new `test-run` subcommand, running `.claude/scripts/dso test-run start` should produce an actionable error directing the user to run `dso update-artifacts`, not a raw `command not found`. The plugin version bump in the same commit as S6 triggers `update-artifacts` detection on next invocation, so host projects naturally sync. COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md documents this upgrade path.
**Why**: Host projects can be out of sync with the plugin version. A silent `command not found` sends users on a wild goose chase; a targeted error with the right next action is the difference between a 30-second fix and 20 minutes of debugging.
**Scope**:
- IN: shim error message for unknown `test-run` subcommand, plugin version bump, one-line note in COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md
- OUT: automated host-project update (users run `dso update-artifacts` themselves)

### Done Definitions
- When this story is complete, running `.claude/scripts/dso test-run start` with an outdated shim returns an actionable error directing the user to run `dso update-artifacts` instead of a raw `command not found`
  ← Satisfies: "after upgrading the plugin, a host project that runs .claude/scripts/dso test-run start with an outdated shim receives an actionable error directing the user to run dso update-artifacts, not a raw command not found"
- When this story is complete, the plugin version in `plugin.json` is bumped in the same commit as the S6 migration, triggering `update-artifacts` detection on next invocation in host projects
  ← Satisfies: "The plugin version is bumped in the same commit so existing update-artifacts detection triggers on next invocation"
- When this story is complete, COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md documents the upgrade path with a one-line note
  ← Satisfies: "COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md documents this upgrade path"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

### Considerations
- [Maintainability] shim error plumbing should reuse existing shim error patterns where present; no new error-message framework needed
## Escalation Policy
**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
data.title: As a host-project agent, upgrading plugin surfaces a clear error when shim is stale instead of raw command not found
